reason: Remote iframe bridge spans contracts, platform-web bridge adapter, PlayView, platform-host, and game-card storage context.
Remote bridge protocol types belong in the shared contract boundary if exposed to external play frontends.
Frontend/browser consumers should use shared bridge payload types rather than redefining them locally.
Implementation must place bridge adapter, platform-host coordination, and PlayView changes in the right package areas.
PlayView is a route component and should preserve existing frontend/bridge separation.
Remote bridge uses active save/game-card state and immediate platform actions.
Bridge query/action boundaries, workspace visibility, raw AI debug exclusion, and platform action behavior are documented here.
Authoritative platform/frontend boundary and remote game frontend direction.
Workspace, trace, diagnostics, Agent/Skill, and frontend data boundaries.
Parent design owns cross-child decisions for permissive URL policy, bridge boundaries, and remote workspace writes.
